1414	// TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized verifies that when the agent makes no
1415	// real commits but the worktree is dirty with only harness bookkeeping files
1416	// (e.g. .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*), SynthesizeCommit returns
1417	// (false, nil) and the outcome is "no-changes", not "merged" or "success".
1418	// ResultRev must equal BaseRev. Exercises real git + script harness: the
1419	// directive writes a noise file under the embedded/ pathspec that
1420	// RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit excludes, so IsDirty=true but nothing stages
1421	// and no iteration commit lands on main.
1422	func TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized(t *testing.T) {
1423		workDir := t.TempDir()
1424	
1425		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1426			parent := os.Environ()
1427			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1428			for _, kv := range parent {
1429				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1430					continue
1431				}
1432				env = append(env, kv)
1433			}
1434			return env
1435		}
1436		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1437			t.Helper()
1438			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1439			cmd.Dir = workDir
1440			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1441			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1442			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1443			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1444		}
1445	
1446		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1447		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1448		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1449		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1450		runGit("add", ".")
1451		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1452	
1453		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1454			ID:        "my-bead",
1455			Title:     "Test harness-noise no-synthesis",
1456			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1457			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1458		})
1459		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1460		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1461	
1462		// Capture main HEAD before the run so we can assert that no iteration
1463		// commit or merge advanced main (the fake's mergeCalls==0 invariant).
1464		mainBefore := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1465	
1466		// Directive writes a noise file under .claude/skills, which
1467		// RealGitOps.synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs excludes from staging.
1468		// IsDirty(wtPath) reports true (untracked file in worktree), but
1469		// SynthesizeCommit stages nothing and returns (false, nil) — the
1470		// all-noise worktree case.
1471		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1472		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1473			"create-file .claude/skills/noise.md harness-noise-content\n",
1474		), 0o644))
1475	
1476		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1477		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1478		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, nil, "my-bead",
1479			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1480	
1481		assert.Equal(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome, "harness-noise-only dirty worktree must not produce a synthesis commit")
1482		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
1483		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.BaseRev, "BaseRev must be set to real HEAD SHA")
1484		assert.Equal(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, "ResultRev must equal BaseRev when no real commit was made")
1485	
1486		// merge must not be called when outcome is no-changes: main HEAD must
1487		// not advance and no merge commit must land.
1488		mainAfter := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1489		assert.Equal(t, mainBefore, mainAfter, "main HEAD must not advance when outcome is no-changes")
1490		mergeLog := runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", mainBefore+"..HEAD")
1491		assert.Empty(t, mergeLog, "no merge commit must land when outcome is no-changes")
1492	}

1494	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits verifies that when the agent runner returns
1495	// an error and makes no commits, the outcome is an execution error rather than
1496	// a misleading no-change result.
1497	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits(t *testing.T) {
1498		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1499			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1500			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // no commits made
1501		}
1502		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed"), result: nil}
1503		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1504	
1505		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1506	
1507		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1508		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1509		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1510		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed", res.Reason)
1511		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed", res.Error)
1512		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
1513		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1514	}

1539	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand verifies that a
1540	// non-zero agent result preserves the iteration instead of touching the target
1541	// branch, even if a merge would have succeeded.
1542	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand(t *testing.T) {
1543		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1544			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1545			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent made commits
1546			mergeErr:    nil,        // merge succeeds
1547		}
1548		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed"), result: nil}
1549		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1550	
1551		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1552	
1553		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1554		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1555		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1556		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
1557		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1558		assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls)
1559	}
1560

1574

1576		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)

1578		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
1579		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1580		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", "aaaa1111")
1581	}

445	// TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure verifies that when merge fails
446	// the result is preserved under a hidden ref. Exercises real git plumbing
447	// and the script harness producing an actual conflicting commit — no fakes
448	// for the components under test.
449	func TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure(t *testing.T) {
450		workDir := t.TempDir()
451	
452		scrubEnv := func() []string {
453			parent := os.Environ()
454			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
455			for _, kv := range parent {
456				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
457					continue
458				}
459				env = append(env, kv)
460			}
461			return env
462		}
463		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
464			t.Helper()
465			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
466			cmd.Dir = workDir
467			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
468			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
469			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
470			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
471		}
472	
473		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
474		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
475		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
476		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
477		runGit("add", ".")
478		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
479	
480		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
481			ID:        "my-bead",
482			Title:     "Test execute-bead preserve on merge failure",
483			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
484			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
485		})
486		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
487		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
488		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
489	
490		// Advance main past baseSHA with a sibling commit that writes hello.txt
491		// with content different from what the agent will produce. Once the
492		// agent commits its own hello.txt on a worktree rooted at baseSHA,
493		// Land() must attempt a merge and hit a content conflict, triggering
494		// the preserve path.
495		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"), []byte("sibling-content\n"), 0o644))
496		runGit("add", "hello.txt")
497		runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: sibling conflicting change")
498		siblingSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
499	
500		// Directive drives the real script harness to emit one real commit
501		// touching the same file as the sibling — replacing fakeAgentRunner's
502		// canned Result struct with an actual worker commit.
503		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
504		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
505			"create-file hello.txt worker-content\n"+
506				"commit feat: worker add hello\n",
507		), 0o644))

764		// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.
765		// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the
766		// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.
767		var usageFileRel string
768		if tokens > 0 || costUSD > 0 {
769			usage := executeBeadUsage{
770				AttemptID:    attemptID,
771				Harness:      resultHarness,
772				Provider:     resultProvider,
773				Model:        resultModel,
774				Tokens:       tokens,
775				InputTokens:  inputTokens,
776				OutputTokens: outputTokens,
777				CostUSD:      costUSD,
778			}
779			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.UsageAbs, usage); writeErr == nil {
780				usageFileRel = artifacts.UsageRel
781			}
782		}
783	
784		// Synthesize a commit when the agent left tracked edits without committing.
785		// Only do so for real changes — harness noise paths are excluded. If nothing
786		// real was staged (committed is false), leave resultRev == baseRev so the
787		// outcome is classified as task_no_changes.
788		if resultRev == baseRev {
789			if isDirty, _ := gitOps.IsDirty(wtPath); isDirty {
790				// Build a preliminary result and write it to result.json before
791				// calling SynthesizeCommit. The commit message is then sourced from
792				// the tracked artifact file, satisfying the provenance contract:
793				// "commit-message metadata must be projected from tracked artifact
794				// files, never from ad hoc runtime state" (FEAT-006).
795				// The final result.json is re-written after the commit with the
796				// correct ResultRev, Outcome, and Status.
797				prelimOutcome := ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
798				if exitCode != 0 {
799					prelimOutcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
800				}
801				prelimRes := &ExecuteBeadResult{
802					BeadID:       beadID,
803					AttemptID:    attemptID,
804					WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
805					BaseRev:      baseRev,
806					ResultRev:    "", // unknown until commit is made
807					Harness:      resultHarness,
808					Provider:     resultProvider,
809					Model:        resultModel,
810					SessionID:    sessionID,
811					DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
812					Tokens:       tokens,
813					CostUSD:      costUSD,
814					ExitCode:     exitCode,
815					Error:        agentErrMsg,
816					ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
817					PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
818					ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
819					ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
820					UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
821					StartedAt:    startedAt,
822					FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
823					Outcome:      prelimOutcome,
824				}
825				populateWorkerStatus(prelimRes)
826				_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, prelimRes)
827	
828				// Render the commit message from the tracked artifact file.
829				commitMsg, msgErr := BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(artifacts.ResultAbs)
830				if msgErr != nil {
831					commitMsg = "chore: execute-bead iteration " + beadID
832				}
833	
834				if committed, synthErr := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(wtPath, commitMsg); synthErr == nil && committed {
835					if newRev, _ := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath); newRev != baseRev {
836						resultRev = newRev
837					}
838				}
839			}
840		}
841	
842		res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
843			BeadID:       beadID,
844			AttemptID:    attemptID,
845			WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
846			BaseRev:      baseRev,
847			ResultRev:    resultRev,
848			Harness:      resultHarness,
849			Provider:     resultProvider,
850			Model:        resultModel,
851			SessionID:    sessionID,
852			DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
853			Tokens:       tokens,
854			CostUSD:      costUSD,
855			ExitCode:     exitCode,
856			Error:        agentErrMsg,
857			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
858			PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
859			ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
860			ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
861			UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
862			StartedAt:    startedAt,
863			FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
864		}
865	
866		// Classify worker outcome: task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes.
867		// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) will
868		// overwrite Outcome and Status with the landing decision before output.
869		switch {
870		case exitCode != 0:
871			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
872		case resultRev == baseRev:
873			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges
874		default:
875			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
876		}
877	
878		// Classify failure mode from worker-level signals. ApplyLandingToResult
879		// may refine this with landing-level signals (merge conflict, gate
880		// failure) before the final result is output.
881		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
882	
883		// When the outcome is no_changes, attempt to read the agent's rationale file.
884		// The agent is instructed to write this file (relative to the worktree) when it
885		// determines the bead's work is already present. We read it before the deferred
886		// worktree cleanup removes the file.
887		if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges {
888			rationaleFile := filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel, "no_changes_rationale.txt")
889			if data, readErr := os.ReadFile(rationaleFile); readErr == nil {
890				res.NoChangesRationale = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
891			}
892		}
893	
894		// Record routing evidence on the bead (best-effort; errors are discarded).
895		appendBeadRoutingEvidence(opts.BeadEvents, beadID, resultHarness, resultProvider, resultModel, routeReason, routeBaseURL)
896	
897		// Record per-attempt cost evidence so cost rollup never has to join
898		// against the session index. Best-effort; errors are discarded.
899		appendBeadCostEvidence(opts.BeadEvents, beadID, attemptID, costEventBody{

189	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
190	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
191	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
192	const (
193		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded = "task_succeeded"
194		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed    = "task_failed"
195		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges = "task_no_changes"
196	)
197	
198	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
199	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
200	const (
201		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
202		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
203		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
204		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
205		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
206		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
207		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
208		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
209	
210		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
211		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
212		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
213		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
214		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
215	)
216	
217	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
218	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
219	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
220	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
221		if exitCode != 0 {
222			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
223		}
224	
225		switch outcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
230		case "error":
231			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
232		case "preserved":
233			switch reason {
234			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
235				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
236			case "post-run checks failed":
237				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
238			case RatchetPreserveReason:
239				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
240			default:
241				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
242				// explicitly requested no merge.
243				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
244			}
245		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
246			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
247		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
248			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
249		default:
250			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
251		}
252	}
253	
254	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
255		switch {
256		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
257			return reason + ": " + errMsg
258		case reason != "":
259			return reason
260		case errMsg != "":
261			return errMsg
262		case status != "":
263			return status
264		default:
265			return "unknown"
266		}
267	}

142	// BeadLandingResult records the outcome of the orchestrator's landing step.
143	type BeadLandingResult struct {
144		// Outcome is one of: "merged", "preserved", "no-changes".
145		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
146		// Reason is a human-readable explanation of the outcome.
147		Reason string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
148		// PreserveRef is set when the result was preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/...
149		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
150		// GateResults holds the outcome of each required execution gate.
151		GateResults []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
152		// RequiredExecSummary is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
153		RequiredExecSummary string `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
154		// ChecksFile is the relative path to checks.json when gate results were written.
155		ChecksFile string `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
156		// RatchetEvidence aggregates every ratchet-evaluated gate's evidence for
157		// the attempt. Surfaced separately from GateResults so HELIX and other
158		// consumers can tell apart ratchet outcomes from generic gate failures.
159		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
160		// RatchetSummary is "pass", "fail", or "" (no ratchets evaluated).
161		RatchetSummary string `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
162	}
163	
164	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
165	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
166	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
167	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
168	//
169	// Outcome rules:
170	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
171	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
172	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
173	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
174	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
175	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
176		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
177	
178		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
179		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
180		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
181		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
182			landing.Outcome = "error"
183			switch {
184			case res.Reason != "":
185				landing.Reason = res.Reason
186			case res.Error != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Error
188			default:
189				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
190			}
191			return landing, nil
192		}
193	
194		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
195		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
196			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
197			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
198				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
199			} else {
200				landing.Reason = "agent made no commits"
201			}
202			return landing, nil
203		}
204	
205		// Agent failed but produced commits: preserve without attempting merge.
206		if res.ExitCode != 0 {
207			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
208			if err := gitOps.UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); err != nil {
209				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result ref: %w", err)
210			}
211			landing.Outcome = "preserved"
212			landing.PreserveRef = ref
213			landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
214			return landing, nil
215		}
216	
217		// Evaluate required gates when a worktree path and governing IDs are
218		// provided. EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult is the canonical helper —
219		// shared with upstream callers that want gate eval before submitting

cd /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ca1f533d-20260424T044932-91e76e8d && git commit --no-verify -m "test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits to real git + script harness [ddx-ca1f533d]

Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with a real-git workDir and

classification path (outcome=error, status=execution_failed) instead of a
canned Result struct."

data.text: Committed. The migrated test exercises real git plumbing + script harness driven by a `fail-during 0` directive, preserving all assertions (exit code, outcome, status, empty preserve ref, base rev) while adding a real-git check that main doesn't advance. Pre-commit `go-test` failures were pre-existing env issues (installation acceptance tests needing a built binary / network) unrelated to this migration.
